angles.summary: Recusal by a sitting member does not cure § 1090 because the prohibition attaches to the body's power to make the contract (Thomson v. Call; Stigall).

The spouse's ~$95K salary is imputed to Okafor via community property (Fam. Code §§ 751/760) as the central candidate § 1090 interest, with nexus through 40% budget dependence and 60% historical win rate.
The board seat and the spousal salary are two analytically distinct interests, each requiring its own cure.

Classification of the uncompensated liaison board seat under § 1091.5 noninterest vs § 1091(b)(1) remote interest, including the disclosure/minutes/total-abstention cure and the § 1091(c) no-influence cliff, and whether resignation is required or merely prudent.
The decisive crux: whether the spousal salary fits any enumerated § 1091(b)/§ 1091.5(a) category, requiring pinpoint verification of current statutory text.

The § 1091.5(a)(6) spousal pre-election-employment safe harbor: the spouse's hire date relative to Okafor's first election is the dispositive fact, with the post-election-promotion wrinkle, the narrow reading where the contract directly funds the position, and the absence of any spousal remote-interest category (§ 1091(b)(2) covers only the officer's own employment).

Defense-side threshold argument that a fixed, non-contingent salary from a majority-non-city-funded budget may fail § 1090's 'financial interest' threshold entirely, countered by the point that employment continuity/security is a cognizable interest.

Adopting the RFP framework/scoring criteria is itself part of 'making' the contract under § 1090's expansive construction (Stigall; Millbrae), so disqualification runs from first agenda-shaping and award-only recusal fails.

PRA § 87103(c) source-of-income disqualification via the community-property share of the spouse's income (far over the $500 threshold); PRA recusal cures the PRA problem but PRA compliance is necessary, not sufficient, where § 1090 applies.

Stage-by-stage FPPC materiality/foreseeability analysis under current regulations: named-party per-se materiality at the award stage, realistic-possibility foreseeability at the framework stage, plus the personal-financial-effects theory via the spouse's job.
Whether a nonprofit board seat is a PRA 'business position' at all given § 82005's for-profit definition of business entity.
The public-generally exception is inapplicable to a targeted three-bidder allocation (with the steelman's contrarian argument that it is 'genuinely available' for a facially neutral criteria vote).
The rule of necessity is unavailable because a disinterested quorum and alternative providers exist.
Correct recusal mechanics: pre-decision disclosure on the record, § 87105/Reg. 18707 leave-the-room compliance, quorum non-counting, and zero informal or staff-level contact from inception.

Consequences package: § 1092 voidness ab initio, avoidance and disgorgement with the city keeping benefits received, no quantum meruit offset (Thomson), the ~4-year discovery-based limitations period, and disgorgement reaching officials and private participants.
§ 1097 felony exposure with willfulness meaning knowledge of the facts rather than the law (Honig), plus forfeiture of and permanent disqualification from office.
Obtain formal pre-decision written advice (documented city-attorney § 1090 opinion, AG opinion, FPPC advice letter) as a willfulness shield and enforcement-immunity source.

FPPC's post-2014 jurisdiction over § 1090 itself — § 1097.1 formal advice and §§ 1097.1–1097.5 administrative/civil enforcement (Naive-Sonnet asserts the opposite, wrongly; the panel never corrects or identifies this channel).

HHAP state pass-through overlay: state audit/clawback and disallowance exposure, grant-level conflict-certification requirements, and proactive disclosure to the state grantor rather than letting it learn from the press.

Delegating award authority to a genuinely independent body as a potential path, with the limit that cosmetic delegation fails where the council still appropriates or approves (Fable's county continuum-of-care variant; Sonnet's $2.3M realism check).

Structural remedies as the only fixes robust to either resolution of the crux: award another bidder, Harbor Light sits the cycle out, or the spouse's employment changes/is decoupled from city-contract revenue.

Cure sequencing: the interest must end before the 'making' process begins, a mid-procurement cure requires restarting (re-adopting the framework), and board resignation after the framework vote comes too late.
Sham-change scrutiny: a spouse's resignation timed to enable the contract, with any tacit understanding of return, invites the very challenge it is meant to avoid.
Okafor resigning the council seat as the honest third option if no exception applies.

Levine Act § 84308 (post-SB 1439) screen: check contributions over $250 to any councilmember from Harbor Light officers/directors/agents in the prior 12 months, triggering disclosure, disqualification, and a contribution ban.

Common-law conflict doctrine as an independent prohibition reaching non-financial loyalty interests, developed through the collision between Okafor's Corp. Code § 5231 fiduciary duties to Harbor Light and the duty of undivided loyalty to the city.
Gov. Code § 1099 incompatible-offices doctrine does not apply because a private nonprofit directorship is not a public office.

Redesign the 2019 liaison-seat resolution itself (non-director/non-voting observer, different designee, or reconsidering the liaison model) as the governance-level fix for a recurring structural flaw.
The conflict runs both directions: Harbor Light's own counsel should wall Okafor off from any board discussion of the bid.
Losing-bidder challenge realism: standing and motive under § 1092/CCP § 526a, near-certain bid protest or writ with CCP § 1021.5 fee exposure, plus grand jury and press interest.
Procurement hygiene to insulate the award: independent or third-party evaluation panel, neutral externally sourced scoring rubric, published objective criteria and documented scoring.

Counter-realism on enforcement: challenge channels are structurally dormant and the real danger is a latent voidness claim raised years later by an audit, hostile future council, or opposition research.
Staff will likely pre-shape scoring criteria to the incumbent's profile before any councilmember act occurs — a taint mechanism preceding any vote.
Informal relational capital in a thin three-bidder market survives any formal cure; a doctrinal cure is not a behavioral cure.
Okafor's advice-seeking functions primarily as a personal § 1097 mens-rea shield, protecting her more than it protects the contract from voidness.
Sociological framings: homelessness-urgency as 'social license' suppressing scrutiny, and electoral/media discipline as the real enforcement mechanism driving private overcorrection.
Two-sided political failure mode: the same cautious memo is attacked by service advocates as sabotage and by watchdogs as cronyism.

Execution degradation of hedged advice: clients round multi-condition advice down to the easiest action item, so convert the cure into an ordered, dated, documented checklist plus a council resolution acknowledging residual risk.
Post-performance clawback scenario: disgorgement after the $2.3M is spent is an extinction event for the nonprofit and displaces active clients mid-contract.
Whistleblower-triggered § 1097 criminal referral as a low-probability, high-severity scenario.

Fund-segregation walls fail on budget fungibility — practical economic reality, not budget-line formalism, controls — and a fungibility audit of Harbor Light's restricted-fund accounting is needed before relying on any wall.

Interspousal transmutation agreement (Fam. Code § 850) as a prospective lever removing the community-property basis — meaningful for the PRA, but not a clean standalone § 1090 fix given its functional 'indirect interest' reading.
Factual investigation of the spouse's actual duties: whether the Director of Programs role administers the RFP'd programs or touches Harbor Light's proposal, which would sharply worsen the analysis.
The 'stacking' theory that board seat plus spousal salary combine into a single incurable direct interest — raised, then rejected internally as unsupported by any cited holding.
No after-the-fact ratification: a clean council re-vote cannot cure a contract void ab initio.
Declaratory relief or a legislative amendment adding a relative-employment remote-interest category as the only definitive resolution paths.
Statutory-surplusage canon: reading these facts as per-se direct would nullify §§ 1091/1091.5 in the recurring fact pattern they were written for.
Quantified probability output: Delphi's ~75% convergence, tetlock's 20/16/64 bucket arithmetic and per-crux estimates — false precision over admittedly correlated questions.

Systematic authority-verification discipline: comprehensive unverified-citation inventory, post-2023 FPPC regulation renumbering, mutually inconsistent Honig fact descriptions, and the disputed Fraser-Yamor/Thorpe pulls flagged as potentially dispositive.

panel_unique_assessment: The panel surfaced 14 angles found in neither naive pass, but only one is substantive: premortem's execution-degradation insight (clients round hedged advice down to the easiest action item; convert the cure into an ordered, dated checklist plus a council resolution documenting knowing assumption of residual risk). That is genuine, billable practice wisdom. A second tier has modest value as risk framing: structural's latent-voidness timing point (the real threat is a claim years later from an audit or hostile council), staff-level pre-shaping of criteria before any vote (actionable — instruct staff in writing early), and the mens-rea-shield observation that Okafor's advice-seeking protects her more than the contract, which usefully clarifies who the client is. The rest is padding for a practicing lawyer: sociological riffs on social license and electoral discipline, the whistleblower vignette, the stacking theory raised only to be rejected, the surplusage canon, declaratory-relief/legislative-amendment paths that are irrelevant to the client's timeline, and the probability arithmetic the panel's own noise audit disowns. Critically, the panel's most-emphasized 'unique' output — naming verification of the § 1091(b)/§ 1091.5(a) exception catalog as the single decisive research task — is honest epistemics but not coverage: Fable simply answered it (§ 1091.5(a)(6), hire date, promotion wrinkle, direct-funding limitation), turning the panel's headline deliverable into the gap between 'someone should look this up' and the looked-up answer.
noise_assessment: The panel's noise is substantial and partly self-documented. Delphi restates the same Thomson/Stigall community-property chain seven times across three presentation layers; Fault Line 3 is confessedly a calibration-philosophy dispute with no doctrinal content; tetlock's 20/16/64 arithmetic projects false precision over admittedly correlated cruxes; structural restates its concentrated-interest point three times and pads with sociology. A large fraction of the report is lens-bookkeeping — who converged at what percentage, which lens contests which lens — that has no legal content a lawyer can act on, and the meta noise-audit itself, while candid, is text the reader must process to discount other text. Worse than padding is the reliability noise: the lenses describe People v. Honig's facts in mutually inconsistent ways, the Fraser-Yamor case name is disputed among panelists, and the HHAP administering agency is named inconsistently — the pipeline generated confident-sounding but internally contradictory authority descriptions. It also failed to catch Sonnet-class errors it should police (the FPPC-has-no-§1090-jurisdiction misconception goes uncorrected because no lens knew § 1097.1). The naive passes carry near-zero noise by comparison: Sonnet's one arguable error (nonprofit as PRA 'business position') is at least flagged as a wrinkle, and Fable is dense throughout.
verdict: No — not in the way a lawyer would pay for. Against the stronger model's single pass, the panel is clearly out-covered on dispositive law: Fable found the § 1091.5(a)(6) hire-date safe harbor (the fact that decides the case), the Levine Act § 84308 screen, FPPC's § 1097.1 jurisdiction, the restart-the-procurement rule, and the only developed common-law analysis, while the panel could only flag its own ignorance of the exception catalog as a research task. More damning, the panel failed to retain angles its own worker model produced unstructured — the FPPC materiality analysis, the transmutation lever, procurement hygiene, the § 82005 wrinkle — suggesting the lens apparatus diverted capacity from doctrine into meta-analysis. The panel's real, purchasable value-add is narrow: premortem's execution/sequencing engineering and an honest map of uncertainty, worth something as a risk-management appendix but not as the analysis itself.
